body:
## Review Summary

Both changes are correct. The `drain` approach in `skip_prefix` avoids the tail allocation (the old `[plen..].to_vec()` created a new Vec for the remaining data), and the `.clone()` removal on `self.child` is valid since `NodeRef::compute_hash` takes `&self`. One minor optimization opportunity on the `drain` usage (see inline at line 98).

comments.body:
nit: the intermediate `Vec<u8>` from `.collect()` can be avoided by extending directly from the drain iterator:
```rust
self.already_consumed.extend(self.data.drain(..plen));
```
`Vec::extend` accepts any `IntoIterator<Item = u8>`, so `Drain` works directly — this avoids the temporary allocation.
